final_evidence_units.text: Source basis: PostgreSQL official documentation, MVCC and locking

cases.question: Python instance object 如何保存状态？

final_evidence_units.text: Instantiation calls the class object like a function and creates a new instance object. If the class defines init, Python calls it during instantiation to initialize the instance state.
After a class statement runs, the class object supports attribute reference and instantiation. Attribute reference reads values such as methods or class variables from the class namespace.
Class variables live on the class object and are shared unless an instance overrides the name. Instance variables live on individual objects.
Mutable class variables should be used carefully because changes may be visible through multiple instances.

final_evidence_units.text: FastAPI dependency injection lets an endpoint declare reusable requirements instead of creating every object directly in the route body.
This pattern keeps route functions focused on the HTTP operation while shared concerns stay in small functions.

A dependency can depend on another dependency. FastAPI resolves this tree and reuses results where appropriate during one request.
Nested dependencies make it possible to build layered behavior, such as authentication that relies on token parsing and user loading.
Authentication can also be expressed as dependencies. A route can depend on a current-user dependency, and that dependency can inspect credentials, validate a token, and return the user object.
Endpoints then receive an authenticated user without duplicating security code.
PureLink validates uploads before processing. Supported local demo text formats include Markdown-like text, Markdown, PDF, DOCX, and other configured extensions.
Upload validation also checks size and MIME settings such as MAXUPLOADSIZEMB, ALLOWEDUPLOADEXTENSIONS, and ALLOWEDUPLOADMIMETYPES.
